ring_warning_oak.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_oak.lore: Carved from a branch of a prophetic oak tree, this ring faintly pulses when danger approaches, alerting the wearer before their eyes perceive the threat.

ring_warning_runed_metal.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_runed_metal.lore: The runes inscribed on this metal band are a warning-sense enchantment of military origin, used by scouts who needed to detect ambushes without breaking stealth themselves.

ring_warning_ancient_copper.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_ancient_copper.lore: Ancient copper carries a memory of the earth's danger-sense, and this ring's verdigris-tinged surface grows warm whenever hostile intent comes near.

ring_warning_bone.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_bone.lore: Carved from the bone of an animal with exceptional instincts, this ring channels a residual predator-sense that alerts its wearer to threats before they fully manifest.

ring_warning_twisted_iron.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_twisted_iron.lore: The twisted iron band vibrates almost imperceptibly when danger nears — a subtle but unmistakable signal that experienced wearers learn to read as reliably as a shout.

ring_warning_carved_jade.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_carved_jade.lore: The jade surface of this ring is cool to the touch until danger approaches, at which point it grows almost feverishly warm — a startling contrast that has saved many lives.

ring_warning_rough_stone.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_rough_stone.lore: An unpolished stone ring of obscure origin that quivers against the skin when hostile creatures are near, as though the stone itself remembers being struck.

ring_warning_grey_granite.name: ring of warning
ring_warning_grey_granite.lore: Grey granite holds the patience of mountains, but this enchanted band pulses with urgency when threats approach — the contrast between stone's stillness and its sudden alarm is deeply unsettling to those who encounter it the first time.

ring_searching_silver.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_silver.lore: Silver is said to reveal what is hidden from ordinary sight, and this ring sharpens the wearer's perception of concealed passages and secret doors in the dungeon's walls.

ring_searching_polished_crystal.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_polished_crystal.lore: The transparent crystal band focuses light into microscopic cracks and seams in stonework, allowing the wearer to notice hidden mechanisms that would otherwise be invisible.

ring_searching_malachite.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_malachite.lore: Malachite resonates with the secret nature of stone, and this banded green ring draws the wearer's eye toward inconsistencies in dungeon architecture — the telltale signs of hidden doors and concealed passages.

ring_searching_engraved_pewter.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_engraved_pewter.lore: The engraved patterns on this pewter band are actually a searching-sense formula, activating a passive scanning enchantment that lets the wearer detect what is deliberately concealed nearby.

ring_searching_gleaming_iron.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_gleaming_iron.lore: An unusually lustrous iron ring that, when worn, makes the wearer acutely aware of the wrongness of concealed spaces — doors disguised as walls, pits covered with false stone, traps hidden in plain sight.

ring_searching_pale_ivory.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_pale_ivory.lore: Carved from a tusk known for prophetic associations, this pale ring lends the wearer a persistent awareness of hidden things — a sixth sense refined from an animal's natural vigilance.

ring_searching_mossy_stone.name: ring of searching
ring_searching_mossy_stone.lore: The mossy surface of this ring is actually a colony of sensing spores that have bonded with the enchantment, creating an organic early-warning system for hidden architectural features.

ring_telepathy_sapphire.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_sapphire.lore: The sapphire's deep blue is associated with clarity of mind across many cultures; this ring leverages that association to project the wearer's perception across distance, reading the surface thoughts of nearby creatures.

ring_telepathy_lapis_lazuli.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_lapis_lazuli.lore: Lapis was prized by ancient mind-mages for its resonance with thought-waves; this ring's golden pyrite flecks serve as focusing elements for the telepathic enchantment locked inside.

ring_telepathy_cobalt_blue.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_cobalt_blue.lore: A cobalt-blue ring that resonates at the exact frequency of sapient thought, allowing the wearer to sense the emotional states and immediate intentions of creatures within range.

ring_telepathy_dark_obsidian.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_dark_obsidian.lore: Obsidian is volcanic in origin, but this particular band was carved from a vein with unusual mental resonance properties — wearing it is like having a quiet voice in one's head that whispers the intentions of nearby creatures.

ring_telepathy_moonstone.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_moonstone.lore: Moonstone's eerie inner glow reflects the light of a mind-reading enchantment. Wearers report that nearby creatures' thoughts appear at the edge of their consciousness like half-remembered dreams.

ring_telepathy_twilight_crystal.name: ring of telepathy
ring_telepathy_twilight_crystal.lore: A twilight-purple crystal that exists in an optical state between transparent and opaque, much like the boundary between private thought and spoken word — wearing it erodes that boundary for nearby minds.

ring_regen_emerald.name: ring of regeneration
ring_regen_emerald.lore: The living green of this emerald pulses with regenerative vitality, accelerating the body's natural healing until minor wounds close between heartbeats and major injuries mend in hours rather than days.

ring_regen_peridot.name: ring of regeneration
ring_regen_peridot.lore: Peridot forms deep within the earth under conditions of intense heat and pressure — a forging process that instills the stone with a drive toward renewal that transfers to the ring's wearer as accelerated healing.

ring_regen_serpentine.name: ring of regeneration
ring_regen_serpentine.lore: Named for its snake-skin patterning, serpentine was associated with renewal and rebirth across many ancient cultures — this ring channels that symbolic power into genuine physical regeneration.

ring_regen_verdant_jade.name: ring of regeneration
ring_regen_verdant_jade.lore: Deep forest-green jade carries the slow patient energy of growing things, and this ring channels that vitality into the wearer's body — wounds closing with the steady inevitability of vines reclaiming old stone.

ring_regen_living_wood.name: ring of regeneration
ring_regen_living_wood.lore: This ring was carved from wood taken from a living tree still in the ground at the time of cutting, preserving the living essence of the plant matter. That essence drives continuous cellular regeneration in the wearer.

ring_strength_iron.name: ring of strength
ring_strength_iron.lore: Iron is the metal of physical labor and martial endurance; this simple band channels that essence into the wearer's muscles, granting a reliable increase to raw strength.

ring_strength_steel.name: ring of strength
ring_strength_steel.lore: The superior refinement of steel over iron is reflected in this ring's stronger enhancement — the muscles of the wearer cord and harden noticeably while it is worn, providing meaningful combat advantage.

ring_strength_adamantine.name: ring of strength
ring_strength_adamantine.lore: The densest metal known to mortals grants the densest strength enhancement known to ring-makers; wearers of adamantine strength rings have been known to bend iron bars with bare hands.

ring_strength_mithril.name: ring of strength
ring_strength_mithril.lore: Mithril's strength-to-weight ratio is legendary; this ring enhances the wearer's physical capabilities to a similar degree — extraordinary power delivered without the usual associated bulk.

ring_constitution_ruby.name: ring of constitution
ring_constitution_ruby.lore: Rubies were believed by ancient healers to strengthen the blood and harden the body against illness and injury; this ring activates that belief as literal fact, enhancing the wearer's constitution.

ring_constitution_coral.name: ring of constitution
ring_constitution_coral.lore: Coral grows relentlessly despite the crushing pressures of deep water — this ring channels that stubborn vitality, significantly bolstering the wearer's endurance and resistance to harm.

ring_constitution_carnelian.name: ring of constitution
ring_constitution_carnelian.lore: Carnelian was carried by warriors for courage and physical hardiness since ancient times — this ring makes good on that tradition, providing genuine constitutional enhancement rather than mere psychological comfort.

ring_constitution_garnet.name: ring of constitution
ring_constitution_garnet.lore: Deep garnet vibrates at a frequency that resonates with the body's fundamental vitality; this ring markedly improves the wearer's stamina, hit point capacity, and resistance to environmental stresses.

ring_dexterity_quicksilver.name: ring of dexterity
ring_dexterity_quicksilver.lore: Mercury's liquid swiftness is captured in this ring's enchantment, translating the metal's effortless flow into enhanced reflexes and precision of movement for the wearer.

ring_dexterity_thin_wire.name: ring of dexterity
ring_dexterity_thin_wire.lore: This impossibly fine wire ring represents the pinnacle of delicate metalworking — wearing it transfers some of that delicacy to the wearer's fingers, greatly enhancing manual precision and overall agility.

ring_dexterity_spun_glass.name: ring of dexterity
ring_dexterity_spun_glass.lore: Spun from a single unbroken thread of glass in a process requiring extraordinary patience and skill, this ring imparts the maker's mastery of precise movement to whoever wears it.

ring_dexterity_featherweight.name: ring of dexterity
ring_dexterity_featherweight.lore: So light it can barely be felt on the finger, this ring was enchanted by a master acrobat-mage who wanted to capture perfect physical grace in an item. The result is a dramatic enhancement to the wearer's coordination and reflexes.

ring_intellect_amethyst.name: ring of intellect
ring_intellect_amethyst.lore: Amethyst has been associated with clarity of thought since antiquity; this ring channels that association into a genuine enhancement of the wearer's reasoning capacity and magical aptitude.

ring_intellect_purple_glass.name: ring of intellect
ring_intellect_purple_glass.lore: Purple glass was used in ancient scrying lenses, and this ring carries an echo of that tradition — wearing it sharpens the mind significantly, improving memory, reasoning, and magical power simultaneously.

ring_intellect_arcane_pewter.name: ring of intellect
ring_intellect_arcane_pewter.lore: Pewter mixed with arcane reagents during casting, this ring's dull surface belies its sophisticated enchantment — worn, it noticeably improves the wearer's intellectual capacity and mana reserves.

ring_intellect_prismatic.name: ring of intellect
ring_intellect_prismatic.lore: A ring that splits incoming light into a full spectrum — a metaphor made literal for the wearer's mind, which gains the ability to analyze problems from multiple simultaneous angles. The intelligence enhancement is dramatic and immediate.

ring_wisdom_opal.name: ring of wisdom
ring_wisdom_opal.lore: The opal's shifting internal fire has always been associated with intuition and deeper knowing; this ring enhances the wearer's wisdom, improving quiz timing and heightening awareness of hidden truths.

ring_wisdom_pearl.name: ring of wisdom
ring_wisdom_pearl.lore: Pearls are formed through patient endurance of irritation — an apt metaphor for wisdom itself. This ring bestows the patient acuity that comes from long experience, significantly improving the wearer's judgment and quiz performance.

ring_wisdom_ivory.name: ring of wisdom
ring_wisdom_ivory.lore: Carved from ancient ivory of uncertain provenance, this ring carries a gravity of accumulated experience that transfers to its wearer as enhanced discernment and measured judgment.

ring_wisdom_white_quartz.name: ring of wisdom
ring_wisdom_white_quartz.lore: White quartz is the stone most used in divination traditions worldwide; this ring concentrates centuries of divinatory enchantment into a permanent wisdom enhancement that provides an exceptional boost to the wearer's perceptive judgment.

ring_perception_topaz.name: ring of perception
ring_perception_topaz.lore: Topaz catches light from every angle simultaneously — and this ring extends that quality to the wearer's vision, expanding their effective sight radius in the darkened passages of the dungeon.

ring_perception_amber.name: ring of perception
ring_perception_amber.lore: Ancient amber sometimes contains preserved insects whose compound eyes saw in directions simultaneously — this ring channels that multi-directional awareness, meaningfully increasing the wearer's field of perception.

ring_perception_citrine.name: ring of perception
ring_perception_citrine.lore: Golden citrine focuses light efficiently, and this ring's enchantment does the same for the wearer's senses — dramatically increasing the distance at which they can reliably detect movement and detail.

ring_perception_hawks_eye.name: ring of perception
ring_perception_hawks_eye.lore: Named for the raptor whose vision is legendary, this ring's enchantment provides an almost supernatural expansion of the wearer's perceptual range — at full effect, wearers describe seeing clearly in conditions that would blind ordinary adventurers entirely.

ring_fire_res_a.name: ring of fire resist
ring_fire_res_a.lore: A fire opal set in a band of heat-treated iron, this ring was forged in a volcano's mouth by a smith who worked alongside fire elementals. It makes the wearer's skin shed flame like water sheds off oiled leather.

ring_fire_res_b.name: ring of fire resist
ring_fire_res_b.lore: This iron ring was quenched not in water but in fire itself, permanently altering its magical properties. The wearer's skin becomes dramatically more resistant to flames — a vital enchantment in any dungeon where fire-breathing creatures patrol.

ring_cold_res_a.name: ring of cold resist
ring_cold_res_a.lore: Quartz formed within glacier ice over thousands of years, this ring carries the cold's patience within it — and grants the wearer an immunity to that very patience, becoming impervious to magical cold.

ring_cold_res_b.name: ring of cold resist
ring_cold_res_b.lore: This crystal seems perpetually frosted, yet the wearer's finger never grows cold — the ring absorbs cold energy before it can harm its owner, leaving the wearer comfortable while ice storms rage around them.

ring_shock_res_a.name: ring of shock resist
ring_shock_res_a.lore: Copper is electricity's preferred path, and this ring was enchanted to make itself that path instead of the wearer — electrical energy flows harmlessly through the ring and dissipates, sparing the body entirely.

ring_shock_res_b.name: ring of shock resist
ring_shock_res_b.lore: This brass ring occasionally emits small spontaneous sparks — a sign that it is actively absorbing and discharging ambient electrical energy harmlessly, protecting the wearer from lightning and shock attacks.

ring_poison_res_a.name: ring of poison resist
ring_poison_res_a.lore: Carved in the shape of a serpent devouring its own tail, this jade ring was used by venomous creature handlers who needed reliable protection against accidental bites. The antitoxin enchantment is thoroughly tested.

ring_poison_res_b.name: ring of poison resist
ring_poison_res_b.lore: Tourmaline naturally concentrates certain minerals that neutralize biological toxins; this enchanted ring magnifies that property dramatically, making its wearer virtually immune to ingested, injected, and contact poisons.

ring_sleep_res_a.name: ring of sleep resist
ring_sleep_res_a.lore: Carved from dreamstone — a mineral that forms only in locations where many creatures have died in their sleep — this ring paradoxically protects against the very sleep effects its material evokes.

ring_sleep_res_b.name: ring of sleep resist
ring_sleep_res_b.lore: Those who wear this pale lavender crystal ring report that their sleep is uncommonly vivid and lucid — but more importantly, sleep-inducing magic simply fails to take hold while the ring is worn.

ring_magic_res_a.name: ring of magic resist
ring_magic_res_a.lore: Every millimeter of this pewter ring's surface is engraved with layered warding formulae, creating a persistent anti-magical field that deflects directed spells before they can take full effect on the wearer.

ring_drain_res_a.name: ring of drain resist
ring_drain_res_a.lore: Carved from jasper found in an old graveyard where necromancers had worked, this ring has developed a natural hostility to life-draining magic — it wraps the wearer's vital energy in a protective shell impervious to drain effects.

ring_levitate.name: ring of levitating
ring_levitate.lore: This ring seems to float on the finger rather than sit against the skin — a subtle preview of the levitation it grants. Wearers drift silently above pit traps and submerged floors, untouchable by ground-level threats.

ring_invisible.name: ring of invisible
ring_invisible.lore: Enchanted with a light-bending field that makes the wearer invisible to normal sight. The ring itself becomes invisible along with everything it touches — veteran wearers learn to keep close track of where they left it.

ring_hasted.name: ring of hasted
ring_hasted.lore: A haste enchantment locked permanently into tourmaline by a wizard who could never tolerate moving at normal speed. Wearers move at doubled pace, making combat and exploration dramatically more efficient — though hunger accelerates equally.

ring_clairvoy.name: ring of clairvoyant
ring_clairvoy.lore: This near-black onyx ring makes the wearer aware of the entire dungeon level simultaneously — not as a map, but as a felt spatial awareness, like having eyes in every wall. It is profoundly disorienting at first and profoundly useful once mastered.

ring_displace.name: ring of displacement
ring_displace.lore: A displacement effect makes the wearer appear to be slightly offset from their actual position — enemies targeting the image find their attacks passing through empty air while the real body remains untouched.

ring_curse_teleport.name: ring of teleportitis
ring_curse_teleport.lore: A cursed ring with a broken teleportation enchantment that activates randomly rather than on command. The wearer finds themselves suddenly elsewhere at unpredictable intervals — occasionally useful, often catastrophic.

ring_curse_aggravate.name: ring of aggravation
ring_curse_aggravate.lore: This cursed ring emits a low-frequency magical irritant that drives nearby creatures to aggression toward the wearer — even docile monsters become hostile, and hostile ones become frenzied. Removing it requires magical assistance.

ring_curse_fumble.name: ring of fumbling
ring_curse_fumble.lore: A maliciously enchanted band that subtly disrupts motor coordination — objects slip from the wearer's grasp, footfalls become uncertain, and combat becomes a series of embarrassing near-misses. Cursed upon first contact.

ring_curse_slow.name: ring of slowing
ring_curse_slow.lore: This ring seems heavier than iron has any right to be, and that heaviness extends to every movement of the wearer — slowed to half speed, they become easy prey for anything with patience enough to follow them.

amulet_warning_leather.name: amulet of warning
amulet_warning_leather.lore: A simple tooth or claw strung on a leather cord, charged with a warning-sense enchantment by a dungeon shaman. The amulet grows hot against the sternum when danger approaches from any direction.

amulet_warning_copper.name: amulet of warning
amulet_warning_copper.lore: An oxidized copper pendant hanging from a matching chain, enchanted to vibrate at the frequency of approaching hostility. The verdigris darkens further as threats draw closer — a colorful if morbid safety gauge.

amulet_searching_bone.name: amulet of searching
amulet_searching_bone.lore: Carved from the small bones of a creature known for finding hidden caches of food, this amulet extends that natural searching ability to the wearer — detecting hidden doors and secret passages that would otherwise go unnoticed.

amulet_searching_bronze.name: amulet of searching
amulet_searching_bronze.lore: A bronze pendant engraved with a magnifying lens motif, this amulet continually scans the environment for concealed architectural features — its wearer detects hidden doors, traps, and passages with unusual reliability.

amulet_telepathy_silver.name: amulet of telepathy
amulet_telepathy_silver.lore: A polished silver disc on a delicate chain, engraved with a mind's-eye motif. The silver amplifies the wearer's natural sensitivity to others' thoughts, making nearby creatures' intentions transparent to anyone skilled enough to interpret the impressions.

amulet_telepathy_jade.name: amulet of telepathy
amulet_telepathy_jade.lore: Shaped like a stylized brain in green jade, this pendant was the prize possession of a mind-mage who claimed to have used it to read dragons' dreams. The telepathic enchantment is genuine and powerful.

amulet_regen_jade.name: amulet of regeneration
amulet_regen_jade.lore: Carved in the shape of entwined healing herbs, this vivid green jade pendant pulses with restorative energy that continuously repairs the wearer's body — minor cuts closing in moments, deeper wounds mending with remarkable speed.

amulet_hasted.name: amulet of speed
amulet_hasted.lore: Forged by an artificer who studied the flight patterns of swifts, this amulet permanently accelerates the wearer's body to the edge of what flesh can sustain — every action taken at double the normal speed, every threat met and passed before slower opponents can react.

amulet_invis.name: amulet of invisibility
amulet_invis.lore: This pendant phases its wearer partially out of the visible light spectrum, making them effectively invisible to most creatures. The enchantment is more complete than ring versions — even shadows are suppressed.

amulet_levitate.name: amulet of levitation
amulet_levitate.lore: An amulet forged from a meteorite fragment that fell slowly — impossibly slowly — to earth over the course of three days. The anti-gravity essence locked in that iron now raises the wearer silently above the dungeon floor.

amulet_clairvoyant.name: amulet of clairvoyance
amulet_clairvoyant.lore: An eye carved in dark stone, this amulet grants awareness of the entire dungeon level simultaneously — a gift that feels like omniscience and would be mistaken for madness by anyone who didn't understand its nature.

amulet_displace.name: amulet of displacement
amulet_displace.lore: A constant visual distortion surrounds whoever wears this amulet — their apparent position offset from their actual location, making aimed attacks frustratingly inaccurate. Enemies targeting the image consistently find nothing but air.

amulet_fire_res.name: amulet of fire resistance
amulet_fire_res.lore: This amulet was worn by a fire-dancer who performed within actual bonfires for audiences. The enchantment protecting her skin has been maintained and strengthened into a reliable and permanent fire resistance.

amulet_cold_res.name: amulet of cold resistance
amulet_cold_res.lore: A locket that is always slightly cold to the touch, as though it absorbs ambient warmth — in exchange, it provides the wearer complete protection from freezing temperatures and cold-based magical attacks.

amulet_shock_res.name: amulet of shock resistance
amulet_shock_res.lore: Made by a guild of storm-riders who needed protection during electrified weather events, this amulet wraps the wearer in a grounding field that routes electrical energy harmlessly into the surrounding stonework.

amulet_poison_res.name: amulet of poison resistance
amulet_poison_res.lore: A genuine serpent tooth from a venomous snake, set in silver and enchanted with an antitoxin field. The irony of using a poison-delivery mechanism as poison protection was apparently not lost on its maker.

amulet_sleep_res.name: amulet of sleep resistance
amulet_sleep_res.lore: Enchanted by a monk who had taken a vow of wakefulness, this medallion permanently keeps its wearer from being magically induced into sleep — mundane exhaustion still applies, but no magical somnolence can take hold.

amulet_magic_res.name: amulet of magic resistance
amulet_magic_res.lore: Woven from threads of anti-magical material and charged with layered warding enchantments, this amulet provides broad-spectrum resistance to directed spells — mage opponents find their carefully prepared attacks losing significant potency.

amulet_drain_res.name: amulet of drain resistance
amulet_drain_res.lore: Forged to protect against the vampiric magics that undead creatures employ, this amulet wraps the wearer's life force in a protective shell impervious to energy drain — an essential item for those venturing into undead-heavy dungeon levels.

amulet_strength_iron_medallion.name: amulet of strength
amulet_strength_iron_medallion.lore: A heavy iron medallion worn by gladiators in the old arenas, enchanted to significantly enhance physical strength. The enhancement is visible — veins cord against the skin and muscles visibly bulk while the amulet is worn.

amulet_strength_titan_iron_pendant.name: amulet of strength
amulet_strength_titan_iron_pendant.lore: Named for the mythological giants who could move mountains, this amulet provides a dramatic strength enhancement that leaves the wearer capable of feats of raw power that should be physically impossible for a creature their size.

amulet_constitution_ruby_pendant.name: amulet of constitution
amulet_constitution_ruby_pendant.lore: A blood-red ruby pendant that resonates with the body's vital force, significantly enhancing the wearer's constitution — their hit points increase, their stamina improves, and minor poisons affect them less severely.

amulet_constitution_guardian_ruby_locket.name: amulet of constitution
amulet_constitution_guardian_ruby_locket.lore: This deep crimson locket houses a guardian spirit that constantly reinforces the wearer's physical vitality — the constitution boost is dramatic enough that seasoned fighters notice it immediately as a transformation in how punishment their body can absorb.

amulet_dexterity_quicksilver_charm.name: amulet of dexterity
amulet_dexterity_quicksilver_charm.lore: A small charm containing a sealed vial of actual mercury, enchanted to transfer its liquid swiftness to the wearer — movements become fluid and precise, reflexes sharpen, and armor class improves noticeably.

amulet_dexterity_air_light_locket.name: amulet of dexterity
amulet_dexterity_air_light_locket.lore: So light that wearing it feels like wearing nothing at all, this air-enchanted locket translates its own near-weightlessness to the wearer's movements — they become preternaturally agile, their every motion flowing with the ease of breath.

amulet_intellect_amethyst_talisman.name: amulet of intellect
amulet_intellect_amethyst_talisman.lore: An amethyst carved into a stylized brain, hung on a silver chain — a fanciful design that nonetheless delivers genuine intellectual enhancement, improving the wearer's reasoning, memory, and magical capacity significantly.

amulet_intellect_brilliant_crystal_medal.name: amulet of intellect
amulet_intellect_brilliant_crystal_medal.lore: Faceted crystal that catches every available light and throws it back in a dozen directions — a fitting metaphor for the dramatically enhanced intellect this medallion provides. Scholars who have worn it describe the experience as thinking with an extra layer of mind.

amulet_wisdom_opal_locket.name: amulet of wisdom
amulet_wisdom_opal_locket.lore: An opal locket that opens to reveal nothing — the container is the enchantment, and what it holds is the distilled wisdom of its original owner, ready to be borrowed by whoever wears it.

amulet_wisdom_sage_ivory_locket.name: amulet of wisdom
amulet_wisdom_sage_ivory_locket.lore: Carved from the cane of a legendary sage who spent forty years in a dungeon, emerging with knowledge of its every secret, this locket passes on a fragment of that accumulated dungeon-wisdom — dramatically improving judgment, quiz timing, and intuitive threat assessment.

amulet_perception_eagle_eye_pendant.name: amulet of perception
amulet_perception_eagle_eye_pendant.lore: An eagle's eye set in gold, enchanted to share the raptor's legendary visual acuity with the wearer — their effective sight radius expands significantly, allowing them to spot threats and targets at distances that would challenge even keen natural vision.

amulet_perception_hawk_feather_talisman.name: amulet of perception
amulet_perception_hawk_feather_talisman.lore: Three hawk feathers bound in copper wire and charged with an expanding-sight enchantment, this talisman dramatically increases the wearer's perceptual range — at full effect, they can monitor a radius of dungeon that most adventurers would consider an entire floor.

amulet_curse_teleport.name: amulet of teleportitis
amulet_curse_teleport.lore: A ruined teleportation amulet whose control rune has been destroyed, leaving only random teleportation at unpredictable intervals. The wearer finds themselves elsewhere without warning — an effect that is occasionally life-saving and frequently catastrophic.

amulet_curse_slow.name: amulet of slowing
amulet_curse_slow.lore: A cursed medallion that makes the wearer feel as though every movement is performed underwater — half speed at all times, reactions delayed, and the frustrating sensation that everything around them is moving normally while they struggle through invisible resistance.

philosophers_amulet.name: Philosopher's Amulet
philosophers_amulet.lore: This golden amulet is engraved with the ouroboros — the serpent devouring its own tail, symbol of eternal knowledge. It is said to have belonged to the first philosopher who descended into the dungeon seeking ultimate truth. The amulet resonates with philosophical understanding, allowing its wearer to identify the true nature of unknown items through rigorous examination. As one of the three pieces of the Philosopher's Set, wearing it alongside the Philosopher's Ring and the Diamond Staff of Inquiry is said to grant the ability to identify items simply by touching them.

dragonslayer_ring.name: Dragonslayer's Ring
dragonslayer_ring.lore: Forged from gold salvaged from the hoard of a great wyrm and quenched in dragon's blood, this ring radiates intense warmth. As one of the three pieces of the Dragonslayer's Set, wearing it alongside the Dragonbone Longsword and Dragonscale Armor grants immunity to fear and +2 to all attacks against dragons — the complete set turns its wearer into the most feared dragon hunter who ever lived.

shadow_walker_ring.name: Shadow Walker's Ring
shadow_walker_ring.lore: A matte-black ring that seems to absorb light from its surroundings, worn by the leader of an ancient thieves' guild who specialized in assassinations too dangerous for ordinary operatives. As one of the three pieces of the Shadow Walker's Set, wearing it alongside the Adamantine Dagger and the Dragonscale Cloak grants a poison strike on all attacks and the ability to move silently regardless of armor worn.

philosophers_ring.name: Philosopher's Ring
philosophers_ring.lore: Inscribed with a miniature version of the Philosopher's Amulet's ouroboros, this ring was worn by the original dungeon philosopher on the hand that held their quill. As one of the three pieces of the Philosopher's Set, wearing it alongside the Philosopher's Amulet and the Diamond Staff of Inquiry is said to grant the ability to identify items simply by touching them — knowledge flowing naturally as breath.

ring_protection_silver.name: ring of protection +1
ring_protection_silver.lore: A silver ring engraved with overlapping ward-circles. The protection it offers is modest but reliable — every experienced adventurer keeps at least one.

ring_protection_gold.name: ring of protection +2
ring_protection_gold.lore: A finely wrought golden ring set with nested protection glyphs. The field it generates slightly deflects incoming attacks, as if the air itself becomes briefly solid around the wearer.

ring_protection_adamantine.name: ring of protection +3
ring_protection_adamantine.lore: A dense band of adamantine inscribed with protection formulae so complex that identifying the ring correctly requires advanced philosophical training. The field it generates is nearly impenetrable.

ring_speed_quicksilver.name: ring of speed
ring_speed_quicksilver.lore: Quicksilver rings that vibrate at a specific resonance can entrain the wearer's nervous system to fire slightly faster. The effect is subtle but cumulative — the ring's owner moves, thinks, and reacts just quickly enough to survive situations that would kill others.

ring_sustenance_amber.name: ring of sustenance
ring_sustenance_amber.lore: An amber ring containing a preserved insect that has not aged for ten thousand years. The preservation field extends to the wearer's metabolism, slowing hunger and reducing the need for rest. Useful in deep dungeons where food is scarce.

ring_strength_dragonbone.name: ring of giant strength
ring_strength_dragonbone.lore: Carved from the finger-knuckle of a fire giant, this ring channels a fraction of the creature's legendary strength into the wearer. The inscription reads: 'Take from strength; do not merely borrow it.'

ring_constitution_diamond.name: ring of endurance
ring_constitution_diamond.lore: A diamond ring that channels the stone's indomitable hardness into the wearer's constitution. Those who have worn it for long periods report an unusual inability to tire, a reduced need for sleep, and a strange clarity of purpose.

ring_intellect_prismatic_deep.name: ring of brilliance
ring_intellect_prismatic_deep.lore: The prismatic ring contains a miniature aurora borealis in its setting — an observable phenomenon with no satisfactory mundane explanation. Wearers report accelerated thought, improved memory consolidation, and an occasional overwhelming sense of suddenly understanding something they cannot articulate.

amulet_life_saving.name: amulet of life saving
amulet_life_saving.lore: A single-use death ward forged from the solidified moment of a healer's last breath. The amulet cannot be refilled — when it triggers, it is spent. But the one time it matters, no price seems too high. Necromancers have offered fortunes to study how this effect works; none have succeeded.

amulet_spell_turning.name: amulet of spell turning
amulet_spell_turning.lore: A medallion inscribed with a möbius-strip ward that sends certain spells back to their casters. The effect is imperfect — complex magics may partially deflect, partially turn, or simply fail. But the look on an enemy wizard's face when their own fireball returns is considered adequate compensation.

amulet_truesight.name: amulet of true sight
amulet_truesight.lore: A lens of perfectly clear crystal that has been inscribed with wards against illusion, displacement, and invisibility. Looking through it reveals the world as it actually is. The feeling of wearing it has been compared to taking off glasses you did not know you were wearing.

amulet_reflection.name: amulet of reflection
amulet_reflection.lore: The face of this medallion is a perfect silver mirror. When hostile magic contacts it, the amulet reflects a portion of the effect back at its source. Powerful spellcasters who encounter this amulet for the first time sometimes cast their second spell into their own reflection.

amulet_titan_constitution.name: amulet of titan constitution
amulet_titan_constitution.lore: Forged in the crater where a titan fell, this amulet radiates a palpable sense of physical authority. The CON bonus it provides is extraordinary — those who wear it describe simultaneously feeling that their body could survive almost anything and that it probably will have to.

amulet_archmage_intellect.name: amulet of archmage intellect
amulet_archmage_intellect.lore: This amulet was reportedly the property of a council of arch-mages who each contributed one year of their own intelligence to its enchantment. The cumulative effect of several lifetimes of accumulated intellect is significant.

ring_curse_drain.name: ring of life drain
ring_curse_drain.lore: This ring appears valuable and may even feel empowering when first worn. The draining effect is subtle at first — a slight weariness after long use. By the time the wearer notices the full extent of the damage, they have usually worn it for some time.

amulet_curse_drain.name: amulet of doom
amulet_curse_drain.lore: The amulet of doom does not harm the wearer directly. Instead, it makes the universe slightly more hostile — doors that would close slowly now close quickly; enemies that might have hesitated now strike immediately; saving throws always seem to land on the worst possible number. It has no off switch.

ring_of_gyges.name: Ring of Gyges
ring_of_gyges.unidentified_name: a plain iron ring with no markings
ring_of_gyges.lore: Plato's thought experiment made physical. A shepherd found it in a chasm opened by earthquake, wore it, discovered its power, seduced the queen, killed the king, and took the throne. Plato used it to ask: would a just man behave differently if invisible? The Ring doesn't answer the question. It only enables it.

ring_of_the_nibelung.name: Ring of the Nibelung
ring_of_the_nibelung.unidentified_name: a heavy gold ring with a tiny rune
ring_of_the_nibelung.lore: Andvari's gold ring, stolen by Loki and given as wergild to the giant Hreidmar. Andvari cursed it: it would destroy every owner. Hreidmar was killed by his son. Fafnir killed his brother for it. Sigurd killed Fafnir. The ring went to Gudrun, who murdered Sigurd. Wagner compressed this into four operas. The curse required no compression.

andvaranaut.name: Andvaranaut
andvaranaut.unidentified_name: a golden ring that seems lighter than it should
andvaranaut.lore: Andvari's magic ring that could find gold wherever it lay hidden. Loki stole it with the rest of the dwarf's hoard to pay the wergild for killing Otr. Andvari cursed both the ring and all the gold as he surrendered them. The ring found gold. It could not find safe owners.

draupnir.name: Draupnir
draupnir.unidentified_name: a golden arm-ring of extraordinary weight
draupnir.lore: Odin's ring — every ninth night eight rings of equal weight dripped from it. Odin placed it on Baldur's funeral pyre. Baldur returned it from Hel as proof that he had been there. Hermod carried it back. It is the only object ever to make a round trip to the realm of the dead and return unchanged.

ring_of_solomon.name: Seal of Solomon
ring_of_solomon.unidentified_name: a signet ring with a six-pointed star
ring_of_solomon.lore: The ring that let Solomon command demons and djinn, given to him from heaven. With it he enslaved Asmodeus, the king of demons, and forced him to assist in the Temple's construction. When a demon stole the ring, Solomon was reduced to begging for food until he recovered it. The ring's power depended entirely on who wore it.

ring_of_eluned.name: Ring of Eluned
ring_of_eluned.unidentified_name: a green ring of twisted ivy-metal
ring_of_eluned.lore: The Welsh Arthurian heroine Luned gave this ring to Owain when he was trapped in a portcullis. It rendered him invisible to the castle's inhabitants until he could escape. She retrieved it afterward, which is the appropriate thing to do with a ring of this power. Most people who receive such gifts do not return them.

brisingamen.name: Brisingamen
brisingamen.unidentified_name: a necklace of four interlocked golden torcs
brisingamen.lore: Freya's necklace, forged by four dwarves over four days. She paid each one a night of her company. Loki stole it in the form of a fly; Heimdall fought him in seal-form to recover it. Freya wore it in battle and her tears became gold. The Brisingamen is the most written-about piece of Norse jewellery. The dwarves received no further acknowledgment in the sagas.

talisman_of_troy.name: Palladium of Troy
talisman_of_troy.unidentified_name: a small wooden figure of armed Athena
talisman_of_troy.lore: The Palladium — a carved image of Athena that fell from the sky — kept Troy invincible as long as it remained within the city walls. Odysseus and Diomedes stole it. Troy fell. Later, every major city of the ancient world claimed to have the original Palladium: Rome, Athens, Argos, Sparta. They cannot all be correct. They may all be correct.

eye_of_horus.name: Eye of Horus
eye_of_horus.unidentified_name: a faience amulet shaped like a falcon's eye
eye_of_horus.lore: When Set tore out Horus's eye, Thoth restored it. The restored eye — the wedjat — became the symbol of protection, health, and restored power. Egyptian physicians prescribed it; sailors wore it on boats. The mathematical fractions of the eye's components sum to 63/64. The missing 1/64 was left to Thoth to complete with magic.

scarab_of_khepri.name: Scarab of Khepri
scarab_of_khepri.lore: Khepri, the scarab god, rolls the sun across the sky each morning — the same motion the dung beetle makes rolling its ball. Egyptian amulets shaped like scarabs were placed over the heart of the dead to prevent it from testifying against its owner at the judgment. The heart was considered the source of moral failure. The scarab, apparently, overruled it.

hamsa_hand.name: Hand of Fatima
hamsa_hand.unidentified_name: an open hand amulet with an eye in the palm
hamsa_hand.lore: The hamsa appears in Islamic, Jewish, and Christian traditions under different names — Hand of Fatima, Hand of Miriam, Hand of Mary. The eye in the palm deflects the evil eye. It is one of the few symbols shared without significant modification across the Mediterranean's three Abrahamic faiths. No one disputes it. Everyone claims it.

ouroboros_pendant.name: Ouroboros
ouroboros_pendant.unidentified_name: a silver pendant of a serpent eating its tail
ouroboros_pendant.lore: The oldest image of the ouroboros appears in an Egyptian royal tomb, 1350 BCE. It represents cyclical time, eternal recurrence, the universe sustaining itself. Alchemists adopted it. Jung analysed it. Kekulé claimed the image gave him the ring structure of benzene in a dream. Whether this last part is true, the benzene ring is real.

necklace_of_harmonia.name: Necklace of Harmonia
necklace_of_harmonia.unidentified_name: a golden necklace of impossible craftsmanship
necklace_of_harmonia.lore: Hephaestus forged it as a wedding gift for Harmonia when she married Cadmus — and cursed it, because Harmonia's mother was Aphrodite, who had cuckolded him with Ares. Every woman who wore it suffered catastrophe: Jocasta, Eriphyle (who betrayed her husband for it), Jocasta again, Callirhoe. The necklace destroyed houses. It is still beautiful.

menat_of_hathor.name: Menat of Hathor
menat_of_hathor.unidentified_name: a beaded collar of carnelian and faience
menat_of_hathor.lore: Hathor's sacred necklace, shaken as a rattle in her rituals. Temple reliefs show pharaohs receiving the menat from the goddess as a guarantee of health, fertility, and joy. It was both jewellery and musical instrument. The sound of carnelian beads against each other was, in Hathor's theology, the sound of everything being well.

heart_of_ahriman.name: Heart of Ahriman
heart_of_ahriman.unidentified_name: a deep red gem that seems to pulse
heart_of_ahriman.lore: Ahriman, the Zoroastrian principle of darkness and chaos, is not merely evil but anti-being — the force that unmakes. His heart, if such a thing could be extracted and held, would contain complete knowledge of all things that can be destroyed. This is a great deal of knowledge. The gem is warm. It should not be warm.

tyet_of_isis.name: Tyet of Isis
tyet_of_isis.unidentified_name: a knotted red jasper amulet like a tied sash
tyet_of_isis.lore: The knot of Isis — the tyet — accompanied the dead as written in Chapter 156 of the Book of the Dead: 'You possess your blood, Isis; you possess your power.' It was always made of red jasper or red stone. Red was the colour of Isis's protective power, not of blood. This distinction mattered to Egyptian physicians.

idunn_apple_charm.name: Idunn's Apple
idunn_apple_charm.unidentified_name: a carved jade apple on a silver chain
idunn_apple_charm.lore: Idunn kept the golden apples that gave the Aesir their eternal youth. When Loki allowed the giant Thjazi to kidnap her, the gods began to age. They forced Loki to retrieve her; he turned into a falcon, turned her into a nut, and flew back. Thjazi pursued. The gods lit fires at Asgard's walls and Thjazi burned. Idunn is the one person in Norse mythology who causes a crisis by simply not being present.

caduceus_charm.name: Caduceus of Hermes
caduceus_charm.unidentified_name: a charm of two serpents coiled around a winged rod
caduceus_charm.lore: Hermes received the caduceus from Apollo in exchange for the lyre. It could put people to sleep or wake them, guide souls to the underworld, and — in later tradition — heal. Medical associations adopted it, confusing it with Asclepius's single-serpent staff. The American Military medical corps still uses the caduceus. Doctors point out the distinction; the military finds it difficult to care.

torque_of_lugh.name: Torque of Lugh
torque_of_lugh.unidentified_name: a twisted gold neck-ring of Celtic make
torque_of_lugh.lore: Lugh of the Long Arm wore a golden torque as one of his divine symbols, along with Fragarach and a magic horse. He was the god of skill — every skill simultaneously — because he argued his way past the Tuatha's gatekeeper by pointing out that while they had specialists, none of them could do everything he could. The gatekeeper let him in.

pectoral_of_amun.name: Pectoral of Amun
pectoral_of_amun.unidentified_name: a broad chest ornament of lapis and gold
pectoral_of_amun.lore: Amun, whose name means 'the hidden one,' was invisible by definition — his cult image was sealed inside the inner sanctum of Karnak, and even priests could not see it. His pectoral was worn by pharaohs when making decisions of state. The decisions were not actually made by the pectoral. They were, however, required to be made while wearing it.

ankh_of_ra.name: Ankh of Ra
ankh_of_ra.unidentified_name: a looped cross of gilded bronze
ankh_of_ra.lore: Ra holds the ankh — the key of life — in all his standard depictions. He passes it to pharaohs; pharaohs pass it to other gods; everyone passes it. The ankh's shape may derive from a sandal strap, a mirror, or a feminine symbol depending on which scholar you consult. In Coptic Christianity it became the crux ansata and survived into church iconography. It is the oldest recurring symbol in Egyptian art.

ring_of_gawain.name: Ring of Sir Gawain
ring_of_gawain.unidentified_name: a plain green ring with a small enameled star
ring_of_gawain.lore: In the Alliterative Morte Arthure, Gawain's strength grew until noon, held until three, and then diminished. In the Green Knight poem, he wore a green girdle he believed would protect him, and was ashamed when his small evasion was revealed. The shame was disproportionate; Gawain was the most courteous knight, which meant his lapses troubled him more than other men's crimes troubled them.

ring_of_odysseus.name: Ring of Odysseus
ring_of_odysseus.unidentified_name: an old bronze ring with a ship carved in the stone
ring_of_odysseus.lore: Odysseus had no magical ring. He needed none. He was the man who thought of the wooden horse, who tied himself to the mast to hear the Sirens safely, who invented false identities with the ease of breathing. What he could not solve with cunning he solved with patience. The ring is his, in retrospect — it was always going to be found by someone who didn't go home the easy way.

ring_of_scheherazade.name: Ring of Scheherazade
ring_of_scheherazade.unidentified_name: a ring set with a pale blue stone that swirls
ring_of_scheherazade.lore: Scheherazade told 1,001 stories to postpone her execution. She did not use magic; she used structure — each story nested inside another, every night's tale ending at a moment too interesting to stop. She survived by being more valuable alive than dead. This is the original argument for intellectual labour. She was also, by the end, the queen.

amulet_of_merlin.name: Amulet of Merlin
amulet_of_merlin.unidentified_name: a blue pendant with a moving star inside the stone
amulet_of_merlin.lore: Merlin was the son of an incubus and a Welsh princess, a fact that concerned his contemporaries far less than it perhaps should have. He advised three kings, built Stonehenge (in some accounts), created the Round Table, engineered the conception of Arthur, and was eventually imprisoned in a glass tower or a tree or a cave by his student Nimue. The nature of the prison varies. The imprisonment does not.

ring_of_pythia.name: Ring of the Oracle
ring_of_pythia.unidentified_name: a bronze ring smelling faintly of laurel smoke
ring_of_pythia.lore: The Pythia at Delphi sat over a chasm in the earth and breathed volcanic vapour while speaking for Apollo. Her pronouncements were famously ambiguous: Croesus was told that if he attacked Persia, he would destroy a great empire. He did. The empire was his own. The Oracle was never wrong. It just required careful interpretation.

anklet_of_atalanta.name: Anklet of Atalanta
anklet_of_atalanta.unidentified_name: a delicate runner's anklet of pale silver
anklet_of_atalanta.lore: Atalanta could outrun any man alive. Her father offered her hand to whoever could beat her in a race. The losers were killed. Hippomenes won by dropping three golden apples from Aphrodite; she stopped to pick them up. She did not stop because she wanted the apples. She stopped because losing was the most interesting thing that had happened to her yet.

crown_of_croesus.name: Diadem of Croesus
crown_of_croesus.unidentified_name: a small golden diadem fragment on a chain
crown_of_croesus.lore: Croesus of Lydia was the richest man in the world and knew it. He tested the Delphic Oracle with an absurd question to verify its power before asking whether he should attack Persia. It answered correctly. He attacked Persia. The Oracle had also answered correctly about that. Croesus's error was not the Oracle. It was the interpretation.

collar_of_njord.name: Sea-Collar of Njörd
collar_of_njord.unidentified_name: a necklace of Baltic amber and whale-bone
collar_of_njord.lore: Njörd ruled the winds and the seas and was responsible for the catch of fish and hunters' luck. He married Skadi, who chose him by his feet — the most beautiful feet among the gods — expecting to have chosen Baldur. They lived six days in her mountains, six by the sea, and both were miserable by turns. Nordic marriage counsellors date their profession's origin to this arrangement.

ring_of_percival.name: Ring of the Pure Knight
ring_of_percival.unidentified_name: a silver ring set with a small cup-shaped hollow
ring_of_percival.lore: Percival failed the Grail question because he was too polite to ask what was wrong with the Fisher King. He had been told not to ask too many questions. He followed the instruction and failed. When he returned and asked — Who does the Grail serve? — the king was healed. The lesson of the Grail quest is that courtesy has limits, and sometimes the correct action is to inquire.

ring_of_lancelot.name: Ring of the Fallen Knight
ring_of_lancelot.unidentified_name: a red-gold ring with a small tower engraved on it
ring_of_lancelot.lore: Lancelot was the greatest knight in the world and destroyed the Round Table by being exactly that. His affair with Guinevere was not a secret he kept — it was a secret everyone kept for him because they needed him too much to confront him. The tower on the ring is the tower of Joyous Gard, where he retreated when his virtue finally failed the test he had spent his life avoiding.

seal_of_agrippa.name: Seal of Agrippa
seal_of_agrippa.unidentified_name: a wax seal pendant with planetary symbols
seal_of_agrippa.lore: Heinrich Cornelius Agrippa published Three Books of Occult Philosophy in 1531, systematizing all of Renaissance magic into a single framework: the elemental world, the celestial world, and the intellectual world. He was accused of heresy, employed by kings, and died in debt. His books were the most widely read magical texts of the 16th century. He himself claimed at the end that they were worthless. He was probably not being entirely honest.

philosophers_ring_legendary.name: Philosopher's Ring
philosophers_ring_legendary.unidentified_name: a ring of strange alloy that seems to look back at you
philosophers_ring_legendary.lore: The philosopher does not merely think — the philosopher perceives. Socrates described his daimon as a voice that told him what not to do; he considered this the most useful kind of guidance. The ring was found in the ruins of the Academy at Athens, described in a catalogue written three centuries after Plato's death. The description ends mid-sentence. The catalogue's final page is missing.

amulet_of_pythagoras.name: Tetractys Amulet
amulet_of_pythagoras.unidentified_name: a bronze triangle pendant with ten points
amulet_of_pythagoras.lore: Pythagoras swore his oaths by the Tetractys — the triangular figure of ten points arranged 1-2-3-4 that represented the musical ratios and the structure of the cosmos. His brotherhood at Croton was vegetarian, banned beans, and believed the soul transmigrated between lives. They were also the first people to prove that the square root of 2 is irrational, a discovery so disturbing that Hippasus, who made it, was allegedly drowned at sea.

ring_of_hypatia.name: Ring of Hypatia
ring_of_hypatia.unidentified_name: a thin bronze ring engraved with mathematical diagrams
ring_of_hypatia.lore: Hypatia of Alexandria was the foremost mathematician and astronomer of her age, head of the Neoplatonic school, advisor to the prefect Orestes. In 415 CE a Christian mob pulled her from her chariot, dragged her to a church, and killed her. Her father Theon had raised her to be 'a perfect human being.' What destroyed her was not her imperfection. It was that the people around her could not tolerate the example.

ring_of_thunder.name: Ring of Thunder
ring_of_thunder.lore: Forged in the heart of a perpetual thunderstorm by an Aztec storm-caller, this ring hums with suppressed electrical charge. Lightning that strikes the wearer grounds harmlessly through the stone, while the storm energy sharpens the intellect to razor clarity.

amulet_of_the_titan.name: Amulet of the Titan
amulet_of_the_titan.lore: Cast from ore pulled from the bones of fallen Titans by Hephaestus as a jest, this amulet transfers a sliver of Titanic strength to the wearer. Those with the constitution to bear it find themselves capable of feats of impossible might.

ring_of_the_deep.name: Ring of the Deep
ring_of_the_deep.lore: Carved from a bioluminescent crystal found only at extreme ocean depth, this ring adapts the wearer's constitution to the crushing cold of the abyss. Divers of the Phoenician coast traded entire fortunes for such rings, which allowed descent to waters where no natural body could survive.

amulet_of_insight.name: Amulet of Insight
amulet_of_insight.lore: Handed down through thirty-six generations of oracle-priests at Delphi, this focus crystal was used to receive the god's voice without distortion. The priests believed wisdom was not intelligence but receptivity — and this amulet sharpens that receptivity to inhuman degree.

ring_of_the_assassin.name: Ring of the Assassin
ring_of_the_assassin.lore: The signet of the Hashshashin grandmaster, recovered after the sack of Alamut. The ring's stone refracts light around the wearer with unsettling precision. Paired with the supernatural quickness it grants, its bearer becomes effectively impossible to track in dim light.

amulet_of_fortitude.name: Amulet of Fortitude
amulet_of_fortitude.lore: A gift from Asclepius to a mortal champion whose constitution had been ravaged by poison. The carved serpent at its centre channels a fraction of divine healing energy into the wearer continuously. Wounds knit, poisons fade, and exhaustion retreats in its presence.

ring_of_far_sight.name: Ring of Far Sight
ring_of_far_sight.lore: Fashioned by the eagle-eyed sentinels of the Watchtower of Rhodes, worn by scouts who needed to perceive threats at inhuman range. The iris of its gem dilates and contracts like an eye, and something in that motion transmits itself to the wearer — both sight and instinct are magnified.

amulet_of_the_archmage.name: Amulet of the Archmage
amulet_of_the_archmage.lore: The ceremonial amulet of the Dean of the Arcane Faculty — a position first held by Thales, who wore it when demonstrating that fire could not harm a mind that understood its nature. Since then it has passed through the hands of thirty-seven archmages, each adding a layer of heat-resistant enchantment.

ring_of_displacement.name: Ring of Displacement
ring_of_displacement.lore: The gem in this ring is a shard of a displacer beast's crystallized nervous tissue — the organ responsible for the beast's light-bending ability. It projects a constant false image approximately one step from the wearer's true position, causing every attack to land on air.

amulet_of_protection.name: Amulet of Protection
amulet_of_protection.lore: Blessed by the last high priest of Esagila, the great ziggurat of Babylon, this amulet projects an invisible ward that absorbs and dissipates magical energy before it can take hold. Even the most precisely aimed enchantments fray and fail against its steady resistance.
